subject: Public Health
title: School Feeding Programs
description: A 60-second lesson on School Feeding Programs.

hook.text: A school meal costing 25 cents can increase enrollment, improve nutrition, and boost learning all at once.

buildup.text: 388 million children receive school meals worldwide—making school feeding the largest safety net on Earth.

discovery.text: School meals reduce absenteeism by 8% and increase math and reading scores—hungry children can't concentrate.

twist.text: 73 million of the most vulnerable children—in the poorest countries—still don't receive school meals.

climax.text: Locally sourced school feeding programs also support small farmers—creating a virtuous cycle of nutrition and economy.

punchline.text: Feed a child at school and you nourish both body and mind.

quiz.question: How many children worldwide receive school meals?
